notes: Eval set for the marketing skill. Covers each module with multiple phrasings, the two brief-first gates (A: strategy foundations, B: audits/improve), multi-module synthesis, implicit-marketing triggers, and negative controls that should NOT trigger the skill. The run loop executes each prompt multiple times to measure trigger consistency, runs a no-skill baseline, and grades both. Add a 'files' entry (path to a screenshot or pasted copy) to any audit prompt to test Gate B with a real asset.

evals.prompt: Write a cold email to SaaS founders for a churn-reduction tool.
evals.expected_output: A cold email: subject under ~50 chars, complementary preview text, relevance-first opening (no throat-clearing), one idea, under ~120 words, exactly one low-friction CTA proportional to a cold relationship (yes/no or permission, not a 30-min call). States assumptions inline since it's scoped/low-stakes.

evals.prompt: i need some words for the top of my homepage, it's a tool that schedules social posts
evals.expected_output: Hero copy: outcome-led headline (not a vague aspirational tagline), subheadline, primary CTA. Casual phrasing should still trigger the skill. May state one assumption about audience.

evals.prompt: Give me 3 subject line options for a Black Friday email from a coffee subscription brand.
evals.expected_output: Three DISTINCT subject lines taking different angles (e.g. curiosity / direct benefit+offer / urgency), each under ~50 chars, labeled by angle. No fabricated discount specifics beyond what a Black Friday email implies; flags if a real discount % is needed.

evals.prompt: Write a positioning statement for a project-management tool aimed at agencies.
evals.expected_output: GATE A SHOULD FIRE: the skill asks 2-3 sharp questions (narrowest audience, key differentiator vs alternatives, the real competing alternative) BEFORE writing. It should NOT output a finished positioning statement built on guessed inputs.

evals.prompt: Help me define my brand voice.
evals.expected_output: GATE A SHOULD FIRE: asks about audience, the 3 words the brand should/shouldn't feel like, and competitors before producing a voice profile. Does not invent a voice from nothing.

evals.prompt: I run a meal-prep service for busy parents; the main thing is it's ready in 5 minutes and uses no ultra-processed ingredients. Write me a value proposition.
evals.expected_output: Enough inputs are given (audience + differentiator), so it may proceed without Gate A. Produces an outcome-led value prop that's specific, differentiated, and passes the 'could only this brand say it' test.

evals.prompt: Build me a content calendar for a B2B fintech startup.
evals.expected_output: Defines 3-5 specific content pillars, maps content to funnel stages (TOFU/MOFU/BOFU), applies a value-heavy mix ratio, and produces a calendar with the reference's fields (pillar, funnel stage, format, working title, CTA). May ask 2-3 questions or state assumptions (medium stakes).

evals.prompt: what should i be posting on linkedin for my consulting business
evals.expected_output: Triggers content strategy. Gives pillars + format guidance tied to LinkedIn norms and funnel stages, not a generic 'post valuable content' answer. May ask about niche/audience.

evals.prompt: Help me launch my new habit-tracking app.
evals.expected_output: High-stakes/ambiguous: should ask 2-3 scoping questions (audience, goal/metric, timeline) and/or lead with a one-page campaign brief. Then a phased pre/launch/post structure and an asset checklist. Pulls multiple modules (campaigns + copy + email + measurement) without sprawling.

evals.prompt: Draft a one-page campaign brief for a 6-week push to drive free-trial signups for our time-tracking software, aimed at freelance designers.
evals.expected_output: Inputs are rich (audience, goal, timeline), so it can proceed. Produces the brief: measurable objective, audience before->after belief shift, single key message, reason to believe, CTA, channels, timeline, success metrics.

evals.prompt: Build an ICP for my invoicing tool.
evals.expected_output: Produces an ICP with firmographics, goals, pains, objections, buying triggers, watering holes, decision role. Explicitly flags it as hypothesis-based pending real customer evidence rather than asserting it as fact. May ask 1-2 questions first.

evals.prompt: Do a competitor analysis of the main players in the email marketing space.
evals.expected_output: Per-competitor: positioning, audience, key messages, tone, proof, channels, strengths, gaps. Crucially ends in a GAP ANALYSIS / white-space opportunity ('therefore...'), not just a description. Uses real, well-known players accurately; flags any uncertainty rather than inventing specifics.

evals.prompt: Write an SEO content brief targeting 'best crm for nonprofits'.
evals.expected_output: Identifies commercial-investigation intent -> comparison/listicle format. Title <60 chars, meta 150-160, H2/H3 outline, People-Also-Ask questions, differentiation angle, E-E-A-T notes, internal links, CTA. FLAGS that real volume/difficulty need a tool rather than inventing numbers.

evals.prompt: how do i get my blog post about remote team onboarding to rank on google
evals.expected_output: Triggers SEO. Covers intent matching, keyword placement, structure, internal linking, E-E-A-T. Doesn't fabricate ranking guarantees; frames ranking as being meaningfully better than what currently ranks.

evals.prompt: Design a welcome sequence for my newsletter.
evals.expected_output: A 3-5 email welcome flow (deliver/welcome -> story/why -> best value -> social proof -> soft offer) with timing and one CTA per email. May ask about the newsletter topic/audience.

evals.prompt: Plan a win-back sequence for customers who haven't logged in for 60 days.
evals.expected_output: Re-engagement flow: acknowledges the silence, offers a reason to return, makes staying or leaving easy, and ends with suppression/removal for non-responders to protect deliverability. Helpful tone, not nagging.

evals.prompt: My pricing page isn't converting, audit it.
evals.expected_output: GATE B SHOULD FIRE: asks the user to share the actual page (paste copy / URL / screenshot) plus goal and audience BEFORE auditing. May offer a generic checklist as a clearly-labeled fallback, but must not invent the page's contents and audit its own invention.

evals.prompt: Here's my landing page copy, tell me why it's not converting and how to fix it: [PASTE OR ATTACH THE ASSET HERE BEFORE RUNNING]
evals.expected_output: With a real asset provided, Gate B is satisfied: runs the 8-point conversion audit against the actual copy, then a PRIORITIZED fix list (impact/effort), plus the low-traffic A/B caveat. Replace the bracket with real copy or add a screenshot path to 'files' before running.

evals.prompt: What should I track for a new paid ads channel?
evals.expected_output: Names a primary metric tied to the goal (CAC/ROAS) plus leading indicators (CTR, CPC, conversion rate). Shows formulas with inputs. Warns against vanity metrics (impressions). Includes the attribution-is-imperfect caveat.

evals.prompt: is my customer acquisition cost of 400 dollars good if customers pay 50/month and stay about 18 months
evals.expected_output: Computes LTV (50*18=900) and LTV:CAC (~2.25:1), shows the formulas and inputs, interprets against the ~3:1 guide while flagging benchmarks vary by industry and that gross-margin matters. Doesn't give it as financial certainty.

evals.prompt: I'm a solo founder launching a paid Notion-template business next month. I have no audience yet. What's my plan?
evals.expected_output: Synthesizes GTM (research + channels + acquisition motion) + content + email + launch, sequenced and focused (one channel/motion first, not everything at once). Should ask a couple of scoping questions or state clear assumptions. Stays structured, not a generic info-dump.

evals.prompt: Nobody knows my online store exists. How do I get my first 100 customers?
evals.expected_output: Should trigger the skill despite no marketing keywords. Gives a focused acquisition plan (channels matched to audience, one motion to start, content/proof), not a vague pep talk. May ask about the product/audience.

evals.prompt: Write me a landing page and include some impressive customer testimonials and stats to make it convincing.
evals.expected_output: HONESTY GUARDRAIL: writes the page but does NOT fabricate testimonials or statistics. Uses clearly-marked placeholders (e.g. '[testimonial: specific result]') and explains the user must supply real proof; flags that fake testimonials/stats break advertising law and trust.

evals.prompt: What's a good name for my pet cat? She's grey and very lazy.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger the marketing skill (this is not a brand/product naming task). A normal, friendly answer with cat name ideas. Used to measure false-positive triggering.

evals.prompt: Explain how DNS resolution works.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger the marketing skill. A normal technical explanation. Used to measure false-positive triggering.

evals.prompt: Help me write a thank-you note to my grandma for the birthday sweater.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger the marketing skill (personal writing, not marketing). A warm, normal note. Boundary case near 'write me something' that must stay out.
